steps.title: Spot the paradox
steps.content: The key signal is even though Cruse himself identified as a Black nationalist. This phrase shows Cruse criticized nationalist leaders despite being a nationalist himself—a paradox proving he disagreed even with ideological allies.

steps.title: Connect to his position
steps.content:
The underlined sentence reveals Cruse's position within the civil rights community: he was isolated because he disagreed even with those he nominally aligned with.
A misreads it as prescriptive direction; C treats it as describing internal movement conflict; B contradicts the evidence of his actual antagonism.
That matches choice D—D is correct.
